turns.content:
Leave request process:
- Employee creates a request (Draft)
- Employee submits → status becomes Pending
- Manager reviews: can Approve or Reject
- If Rejected, employee can Edit and resubmit (back to Pending)
- If Approved, HR processes it → Completed
- Employee can Cancel a Draft or Pending request at any time
turns.label: requirement

turns.content: D
turns.label: workflow_selection

turns.content: Yes, correct
turns.label: state_confirm

turns.content: Most requests approved. 10% rejected, usually resubmitted once. Cancellations rare.
turns.label: realworld_data

turn_assertions.check: identifies 6 states: Draft, Pending, Approved, Rejected, Completed, Cancelled — with Draft=Initial, Completed+Cancelled=Final
lists 7 transitions including the Rejected→Pending loop and Cancel from Draft+Pending
does NOT add transitions not stated in requirement (e.g., Approved→Cancelled)
state diagram shows loop and branching, matrix shows Cancel valid from Draft+Pending only
path tests include happy path, rejection+resubmit path, and cancel path
